name: Milk (Cow) (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Milk (Cow) [Milk and milk products] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered (capped at 250).

provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
provenance.verification: expert-curated (multi-source food composition; growth-relevant profile completed, FooDB noise removed)
curation_sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 172217 (SR Legacy: Milk, whole, 3.25% milkfat, without added vit A/D) — full vitamin/mineral/amino-acid/fatty-acid panel
USDA FoodData Central FDC 746782 (Foundation Foods: Milk, whole, 3.25% milkfat, with added vitamin D; 24 samples, 2018)
Variability of citric acid in cow milk composition (ResearchGate 304771887): mean citric acid 133 mg/100 mL
Orotic acid in cow milk: PubMed 1958838 / Agrarforschung Schweiz 2008 (~66 mg/L)
Amino acid composition of cow milk: PMC8535404
Dairy organic-acid HPLC methodology and typical raw-milk values: Marsili et al. 1981 J. Dairy Sci.; Fernandez-Garcia & McGregor 1994 (ScienceDirect S0021967304001311); raw-milk SCFA GC-MS PMC10093571
provenance.curation_notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is LACTOSE (~4.8 g/100 g, ~140 mM); the key correction is adding CITRIC ACID/citrate (~0.16 g/100g, ~8 mM), the dominant milk organic acid that was entirely missing, plus free glucose/galactose, hippuric acid, iodine and biotin. Lactic acid is LOW in fresh milk (rises on fermentation). Removed heavy-metal contaminants (arsenate, cadmium, nickel), an antibiotic residue (tetracycline), ng/L steroid hormones, plant-only compounds (pectin, gallic acid), and a volatile c
curation_added: citric acid (citrate) — dominant organic acid of milk, was entirely absent from current_components
hippuric acid — bovine-metabolite organic acid, absent
free D-glucose and D-galactose with amounts (USDA panel lists lactose only)
quantitative amounts for lactose, all amino acids, vitamins, minerals, and fatty acids

curation_removed.name: Arsenate
curation_removed.reason: non-nutritive toxic trace element / contaminant, not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Tetracycline
curation_removed.reason: veterinary antibiotic residue/contaminant, not a milk nutrient

curation_removed.name: Estradiol c
curation_removed.reason: steroid hormone present only at ng/L trace; FooDB predicted, not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Estriol c
curation_removed.reason: steroid hormone at ng/L trace; FooDB predicted, not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Estrone cytosol
curation_removed.reason: steroid hormone at ng/L trace; FooDB predicted, not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Dehydroepiandrosterone
curation_removed.reason: steroid hormone at ultratrace; not a growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Serotonin
curation_removed.reason: trace bioamine; not a quantitative growth nutrient

curation_removed.name: Pectin C6H7O6
curation_removed.reason: plant cell-wall polysaccharide; biologically absent in milk, FooDB matrix error

curation_removed.name: Gallic acid
curation_removed.reason: plant polyphenol; negligible/implausible in milk, FooDB noise

curation_removed.name: Toluene
curation_removed.reason: volatile hydrocarbon contaminant, not a nutrient

curation_removed.name: Calcitriol
curation_removed.reason: active 1,25-vitamin-D hormone at ultratrace; redundant with vitamin D (cholecalciferol)

curation_removed.name: 25-Hydroxyvitamin D2
curation_removed.reason: trace vitamin-D metabolite form; redundant with vitamin D entry

curation_removed.name: Nickel
curation_removed.reason: ultratrace non-essential element; contaminant-level, not a growth nutrient

components.name: lactose
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 746782 (Foundation, 24 samples) / FDC 172217 (SR Legacy 5.05 g)

components.name: D-glucose
components.evidence_source: Milk composition literature (free glucose ~10-20 mg/100 mL fresh cow milk); USDA reports 0 for the disaccharide-only panel

components.name: D-galactose
components.evidence_source: Milk composition literature (free galactose ~10-20 mg/100 g fresh milk)

components.name: citric acid
components.evidence_source: Variability of citric acid in cow milk (ResearchGate 304771887): mean 133 mg/100 mL, range 95-203 mg/100 mL

components.name: L-lactate
components.evidence_source: Dairy organic-acid HPLC literature (Marsili 1981; Fernandez-Garcia & McGregor 1994, ScienceDirect S0021967304001311)

components.name: pyruvate
components.evidence_source: Dairy organic-acid HPLC literature (~10-20 mg/L raw milk)

components.name: acetate
components.evidence_source: Raw-milk short-chain fatty acid GC-MS (PMC10093571) and HPLC literature

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 172217 amino-acid panel; corroborated PMC8535404

components.name: iodine
components.evidence_source: Milk composition literature / McCance & Widdowson (~15-30 ug/100 g, feed-dependent)

components.name: biotin
components.evidence_source: Milk composition literature (~1-4 ug/100 g); not in USDA panel

components.name: vitamin D
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 172217 (0.1 ug natural); fortified milk ~1.3 ug (FDC 746782)

components.name: vitamin C
components.evidence_source: USDA FDC 172217 (0.0 mg); trace ~0.9 mg in very fresh raw milk

components.name: urea
components.evidence_source: Milk composition literature (milk urea nitrogen; ~20-35 mg/100 g)

components.name: Beta-Carotene, Lutein, Zeaxanthin, Echinenone (genuine milkfat carotenoids)
Glycine betaine, Taurine, L-carnitine and acylcarnitines (genuine minor milk metabolites)
orotate/urate/allantoin and nucleosides (genuine milk purine/pyrimidine metabolites)

uncovered.name: uric acid
uncovered.note: no exchange auto-assigned; propose uptake -1.0 pending manual curation

uncovered.name: hippuric acid
uncovered.note: no exchange auto-assigned; propose uptake -1.0 pending manual curation

uncovered.note: no exchange auto-assigned; propose uptake -1.0 pending manual curation

uncovered.note: no exchange auto-assigned; propose uptake -1.0 pending manual curation

uncovered.name: 6'-Sialyllactose (genuine milk oligosaccharide, no BiGG met)
uncovered.note: no exchange auto-assigned; propose uptake -1.0 pending manual curation

uncovered.name: milk triacylglycerols TG(...) and odd/branched-chain fatty acids (pentadecanoic, heptadecanoic, vaccenic) — genuine ruminant milkfat lipids
uncovered.note: no exchange auto-assigned; propose uptake -1.0 pending manual curation

oxygen_note: food substrate; O2 availability is a simulation condition, not a property of the food
basis: per 100 g fresh whole cow milk (3.25% milkfat)
